title: PAN Card Application
shortDescription: Official service for new PAN card application or data correction for Indian citizens.
eligibility: Any individual, including minors, NRIs, and HUFs.
stepsToApply: Visit the official Protean-TIN PAN portal.
Select 'Online PAN Services' and click 'Paperless PAN Application'.
Register to generate a token number for your application.
Fill in personal and contact details, and choose your preferred PAN type.
Upload required supporting documents, submit the form, and complete the payment.

title: Passport Application
shortDescription: Portal for applying for fresh passports or re-issue of existing passports.

fees.details: Varies by passport type (Normal/Tatkal) and booklet size.

stepsToApply: Register as a new user on the Passport Seva Portal.
Log in and select 'Apply for Fresh Passport/Re-issue of Passport'.
Fill out the application form online and submit it.
Schedule an appointment at the nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK).
Visit the PSK with original documents for verification.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
title: e-Aadhaar Download
shortDescription: Download a digitally signed electronic copy of your identity card.
stepsToApply: Click on 'Download Aadhaar' under the services section.
Authenticate using your Aadhaar number or Enrollment ID.
Verify the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Download the password-protected PDF copy of your e-Aadhaar.

title: Birth Certificate Application
shortDescription: Official registration and issuance of birth certificates for citizens.
eligibility: All Indian citizens born in India.
stepsToApply: Obtain the registration form from the local municipal/registrar office or their website.
Fill in the form with birth details and parent information.
Attach supporting documents including hospital records.
Submit the application to the Registrar's office or via the state portal.
Collect the certificate once processing is complete.

title: Voter ID Registration
shortDescription: Register as a new voter to participate in Indian elections.
stepsToApply: Visit the official Voters' Service Portal or use the Voter Helpline App.
Fill out Form 6 for new voter registration.
Provide personal details, address, and upload the required documents.
Submit the form for field verification by the local booth officer.
Receive your Elector's Photo Identity Card (EPIC) via speed post.

ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
title: Driving Licence Application
shortDescription: Official service to apply for a new Learner's Licence or permanent Driving Licence.
eligibility: Minimum 16 years for motorcycle without gear; 18 years for light motor vehicles.

fees.details: Varies by state and licence class.

stepsToApply: Select 'Online Services' and choose 'Driving Licence Related Services'.
Fill out the application form and upload scanned copies of required documents.
Book an appointment for the learner's test or driving skill test.
Visit the RTO for biometric verification and the driving test.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
title: DigiLocker Account
shortDescription: A platform for issuance and verification of documents and certificates in a digital format.
stepsToApply: Visit the DigiLocker website or mobile app.
Click on 'Sign Up' and enter your mobile number linked to your identity.
Verify your identity using the OTP received on your mobile.
Set a security PIN for account access.
Link your government-issued documents to your digital locker.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: ABHA Health Card
shortDescription: Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) for digitizing health records.
stepsToApply: Go to the official ABHA website.
Authenticate using your identity proof (e.g., [Aadhaar Redacted] or mobile number).
Enter personal demographic details as requested.
Download and save your ABHA card for medical records.

title: Death Certificate Application
shortDescription: Registration of death events for legal and administrative record-keeping.
eligibility: Next of kin or authorized representatives of the deceased.

fees.details: Depends on municipal corporation fee structures.

requiredDocuments: Medical Certificate of Cause of Death
stepsToApply: Report the death to the local registrar within 21 days.
Visit the Civil Registration System (CRS) portal.
Fill in the death report form online with the necessary supporting documents.
Submit the application to the local municipal authority.
Receive the certificate via the portal or at the municipal office.

title: Marriage Certificate
shortDescription: Official documentation certifying the marriage of two individuals under Indian law.
eligibility: Parties must meet the legal age requirements (18 for women, 21 for men).
requiredDocuments: Proof of age and address for both parties
Marriage invitation card or temple/church certificate
Two passport-sized photographs of both parties
stepsToApply: Visit the state-specific Marriage Registration portal.
Fill out the application form for marriage registration.
Upload required identity, address, and marriage proof documents.
Book an appointment for the Registrar's office.
Appear before the Registrar with witnesses for final verification and issuance.

ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
department: Department of Food and Public Distribution
title: Ration Card Application
shortDescription: Official service to apply for a ration card for accessing subsidized food grains.
eligibility: Indian citizens meeting state-specific income criteria.

fees.details: Nominal fee depending on state regulations.

stepsToApply: Visit the state-specific Food and Civil Supplies portal.
Fill in family details and upload required income and address proofs.
Submit the application for verification by the local Food Supply Officer.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
department: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
title: Unique Disability ID (UDID)
shortDescription: National database for persons with disabilities to facilitate universal accessibility.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a benchmark disability.
stepsToApply: Register and fill out the 'Apply for Disability Certificate & UDID Card' form.
Upload medical certificates and personal identification documents.
Application is forwarded to the District Medical Authority for assessment.
Receive the digital card upon successful verification.

title: UAN Generation
shortDescription: Universal Account Number (UAN) for employees to manage their Provident Fund.
stepsToApply: Employer typically initiates UAN generation upon joining.
Activate the UAN on the EPFO Member Portal.
Link your mobile number and identity documents for verification.
Access PF balance and services through the portal.

department: Department of School Education and Literacy
title: APAAR ID Registration
shortDescription: Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry for tracking student academic progress.
stepsToApply: Visit the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) portal.
Sign up using your [Aadhaar Redacted] for e-KYC authentication.
Store and share academic credits digitally.

title: Arms License Application
shortDescription: Official portal for application and renewal of arms licenses.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 21+ with verified credentials.

fees.details: Based on license type and state fee schedules.

stepsToApply: Fill the application form with required justifications.
Undergo mandatory safety training at a licensed facility.
Submit the application for police investigation.
Appear before the licensing authority for final approval.

title: Director Identification Number (DIN)
shortDescription: Unique identifier for company directors in India.
eligibility: Individuals intending to be directors of a company.

fees.details: Applicable filing fees as per MCA guidelines.

stepsToApply: Create a user account on the MCA portal.
Apply for the DIR-3 form online.
Attach attested ID and address proofs.
Submit and track the approval of your unique DIN.

title: TAN Registration
shortDescription: Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number for business entities.
eligibility: Businesses liable to deduct tax at source.

fees.details: Application fee as per NSDL guidelines.

stepsToApply: Pay the fee using net banking or credit/debit card.

title: Udyam Registration
shortDescription: Identity registration for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME).
eligibility: Business entities meeting MSME turnover/investment criteria.
stepsToApply: Enter [Aadhaar Redacted] and name for validation.
Fill in enterprise details and investment information.
Submit for automated verification against PAN/GST databases.

title: GST Identification Number (GSTIN)
shortDescription: Unique registration number for businesses under the GST regime.
eligibility: Businesses with turnover exceeding exemption limits.
stepsToApply: Visit the GST portal and select 'New Registration'.
Complete Part A of the application using PAN/Mobile/Email.
Fill Part B with business details and upload documents.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: Jeevan Pramaan (Life Certificate)
shortDescription: Digital life certificate for pensioners.
stepsToApply: Download Jeevan Pramaan app or visit a service center.
Register using [Aadhaar Redacted] and Pension details.
Certificate is automatically updated in the pension department's records.

title: Caste/Domicile Certificate
shortDescription: Certificate verifying an individual's caste or state of residence.
eligibility: Citizens residing in specific state for required duration.
stepsToApply: Visit the relevant state government service portal.
Fill the form and upload supporting revenue/residency records.
Field verification by local revenue officials.
Certificate issued electronically or via CSC.

title: Legal Name Change
shortDescription: Official process for legal change of name through Gazette Notification.
stepsToApply: Draft an affidavit for name change with a notary.
Publish the change in two local newspapers.
Submit the application through the e-Gazette portal.
Receive the Gazette notification certifying the name change.

title: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)
shortDescription: Registration for OCI card for individuals of Indian origin.
stepsToApply: Upload digital photo and supporting documents.
Submit physical application at the nearest Indian Mission/Post.

title: Succession Certificate
shortDescription: Document certifying the legal heir of a deceased person's movable property.

fees.details: Court fees apply based on asset value.

stepsToApply: File a petition in the District Court or relevant local authority.
Provide public notice regarding the intent to claim.
Court conducts inquiry and invites objections.
Grant of certificate by the judge.
Certificate issued for collection of debts/securities.

title: Railway Identity Card
shortDescription: Identity document for authorized railway employees and beneficiaries.
stepsToApply: Get photo and credentials verified by supervisor.
Processed through the internal rail administrative portal.
Physical/Digital card issued by the department.
Used for travel and duty identification.

title: Police Clearance Certificate (PCC)
shortDescription: Certificate of good conduct issued by the police for immigration or employment.
stepsToApply: Apply via Passport Seva portal or local Police station portal.
Record of any criminal history is verified.
Receive PCC via email or post.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: CGHS Card
shortDescription: Health identification card for Central Government employees and pensioners.
stepsToApply: Collect card from the CGHS wellness center.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
title: Post Office Identity Card
shortDescription: Photo identity card issued by the Department of Posts.
eligibility: Any citizen can apply at designated post offices.

fees.details: Standard fee charged by India Post.

stepsToApply: Visit the local Head Post Office.
Pay the requisite card issuance fee.
Receive the card at the registered address.

ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
title: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
shortDescription: Agricultural credit card serving as both credit tool and identity.
stepsToApply: Visit the bank branch or portal.
Submit application with land ownership records.
KCC is issued to the farmer.

title: Gazette Notification for Correction
shortDescription: Official process for correcting errors in name, DOB, or other details via Gazette.
stepsToApply: Draft an affidavit stating the required correction.
Upload request on the e-Gazette portal.
Gazette issue published with corrected details.

title: Hindi Language Proficiency Certificate
shortDescription: Certificate for government employees demonstrating proficiency in Hindi.

title: NPS Subscriber ID
shortDescription: Unique Permanent Retirement Account Number (PRAN) for NPS subscribers.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NACO Registration ID
shortDescription: Identification for individuals registered under national health outreach programs.

title: ASI Researcher ID
shortDescription: Identity card for scholars researching at protected monuments.

title: NIOS Enrollment ID
shortDescription: Student ID for individuals enrolled in open school education.

title: Pollution Consent ID
shortDescription: Operating consent ID for industrial units.

title: Skill India ID
shortDescription: Identifier for candidates enrolled under PMKVY and skill programs.

title: Emigration Clearance ID
shortDescription: Required clearance ID for ECR passport holders working abroad.

title: Advocate Enrollment ID
shortDescription: Professional ID for legal practitioners in India.

title: Census Enumerator ID
shortDescription: ID assigned to temporary census field staff.

title: Phytosanitary ID
shortDescription: Document ID for export of plant materials.

title: Lab Accreditation ID
shortDescription: Accreditation ID for testing laboratories.

title: IEC Registration
shortDescription: Import Export Code for international trade.

title: Scientist Registration ID
shortDescription: Official registration for researchers in DST-funded projects.

title: Civil Defence Volunteer ID
shortDescription: Identity for authorized volunteers during emergencies.

title: Energy Auditor ID
shortDescription: Certification for professionals conducting energy audits.

title: Customs Broker ID
shortDescription: Authorized identifier for licensed customs brokers.

title: Borewell Drilling License
shortDescription: Operational permit/ID for licensed drillers.

title: Tourist Guide ID
shortDescription: Government-recognized ID for professional tourist guides.

title: Mining Lease ID
shortDescription: Unique registration ID for legal mining lease operations.
stepsToApply: Apply for unique lease ID on portal.

title: Income Tax Return (ITR) Filing
shortDescription: Portal for filing annual Income Tax Returns for individuals and businesses.
eligibility: Taxpayers with annual income exceeding basic exemption limits.

fees.details: No government fee for online filing.

stepsToApply: Choose Assessment Year and ITR form.
Verify and e-Verify using [Aadhaar Redacted] OTP.

title: GST Registration
shortDescription: Registration for businesses to comply with Goods and Services Tax laws.
eligibility: Businesses with turnover above threshold limits.
stepsToApply: Fill Part B with business info.

title: EPF Balance Check
shortDescription: Check Employees' Provident Fund balance and claim status.
stepsToApply: Log in with UAN and password.

title: National Pension System (NPS) Enrollment
shortDescription: Enrollment for the government-sponsored pension scheme.

title: Public Provident Fund (PPF) Account
shortDescription: Open a tax-saving long-term investment account.
stepsToApply: Visit local Post Office or net banking portal.

title: Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGB) Purchase
shortDescription: Government securities denominated in grams of gold.
stepsToApply: Log in to your bank's net banking.

title: PM Jan Dhan Yojana Account
shortDescription: Opening a basic savings bank deposit account.

title: TDS Refund Status
shortDescription: Check the status of your Tax Deducted at Source refund.

title: Professional Tax Registration
shortDescription: Registration for professional tax in relevant states.
eligibility: Employers and Professionals in specific states.

title: PAN Correction
shortDescription: Update details like name or address on your PAN card.

title: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
shortDescription: Pension scheme for unorganized sector workers.
eligibility: 18-40 years old with a bank account.

title: GST Payment Portal
shortDescription: Make online GST tax payments.

title: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
shortDescription: Accidental insurance scheme at a nominal premium.
eligibility: 18-70 years old with a bank account.

title: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
shortDescription: Life insurance scheme for bank account holders.
eligibility: 18-50 years old with a bank account.

title: RBI Retail Direct Scheme
shortDescription: Invest directly in Government Securities (G-Sec).

title: Advance Tax Payment
shortDescription: Pay tax in installments if liability exceeds ₹10,000.

title: TDS on Property (Form 26QB)
shortDescription: Pay TDS when buying immovable property.
eligibility: Buyers of property over specific values.

title: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Loan
shortDescription: Access agriculture credit for farmers.

title: Vivad se Vishwas Scheme
shortDescription: Tax dispute resolution scheme.

title: Correction of GST Return
shortDescription: Amend errors in filed GST returns.

title: TDS Certificate (Form 16A) Download
shortDescription: Download certificates for TDS deducted on non-salary income.
stepsToApply: Download and extract the zip file.

title: Sukanaya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
shortDescription: Small savings scheme for the girl child.
eligibility: Guardians of girl child under 10 years.

title: PAN Application (Form 49A)
shortDescription: Apply for a new Permanent Account Number.

title: GST Refund Application
shortDescription: Apply for a refund of excess tax paid.

title: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate
shortDescription: One-time small savings scheme for women.

title: ITR Verification (e-Verification)
shortDescription: Verify filed ITR to complete the filing process.

title: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
shortDescription: Loans for non-corporate, non-farm small enterprises.

title: TAN Registration (Form 49B)
shortDescription: Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number.

title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS)
shortDescription: Savings scheme for individuals aged 60+.

title: LUT Filing for Exports
shortDescription: Letter of Undertaking for GST-free exports.

title: PM SVANidhi
shortDescription: Micro-credit for street vendors.

title: Link Aadhaar with PAN
shortDescription: Mandatory linkage of your PAN and [Aadhaar Redacted] number.

title: Know Your Assessing Officer
shortDescription: Locate jurisdiction of your Assessing Officer.

title: GST Registration Amendment
shortDescription: Update business core/non-core fields in GST registration.

title: Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS)
shortDescription: Monthly interest income investment scheme.

title: e-Way Bill Generation
shortDescription: Required for goods transport over ₹50,000.
stepsToApply: Log in to e-Way Bill portal.

title: Generate Challan 280
shortDescription: Payment of Advance Tax or Self-Assessment Tax.

title: National Savings Certificate (NSC)
shortDescription: Safe investment tool with tax benefits.

title: GST Cancellation Application
shortDescription: Voluntary cancellation of GST registration.

title: PAN Card Reprints
shortDescription: Request a physical copy of your existing PAN card.

title: GST Annual Return (GSTR-9)
shortDescription: Filing annual financial summaries.

title: Micro Unit Refinancing (MUDRA Card)
shortDescription: Debit card linked to Mudra loan limit.

title: Income Tax Grievance (e-Nivaran)
shortDescription: Resolve tax-related complaints.

title: GST ITC-04 Filing
shortDescription: Reporting of goods sent to job work.

title: View Form 26AS
shortDescription: Check tax credit statement.

title: Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP)
shortDescription: Investment scheme that doubles the principal amount.

title: GST Payment Ledger Check
shortDescription: Verify electronic cash and credit ledgers.

title: TDS Return Filing (e-TDS)
shortDescription: Filing periodic TDS statements.

title: GST Offline Tool Usage
shortDescription: Bulk upload data using GST offline tool.
stepsToApply: Download offline tool from GST portal.

title: PAN Verification Service
shortDescription: Verify PAN card authenticity online.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY
shortDescription: Provides health insurance cover of up to ₹5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
eligibility: Families identified under the SECC 2011 database or those with active Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY) cards.

fees.details: No enrollment fee or premium for eligible beneficiaries.

stepsToApply: Visit the 'Am I Eligible' portal on the PM-JAY website.
Enter your mobile number and verify via OTP.
Search for your name using Ration Card, Aadhaar, or SECC details.
If eligible, visit the nearest empanelled hospital or Common Service Centre (CSC) for KYC.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account)
shortDescription: Create a 14-digit digital health ID to store and share medical records securely.

fees.details: Zero cost for creation and maintenance.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar-linked Mobile Number or Driving License
stepsToApply: Visit the official ABHA portal or download the ABHA mobile app.
Choose authentication via Aadhaar or Driving License.
Enter the OTP received on the registered mobile number.
Fill in basic profile details and download the ABHA card.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: CoWIN Vaccination Portal
shortDescription: Digital platform for scheduling and tracking COVID-19 and other routine immunizations.
eligibility: All age groups eligible for approved vaccines.

fees.details: Free at government centers; fixed costs at private hospitals.

requiredDocuments: Photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, or Voter ID)
stepsToApply: Visit the CoWIN portal or use the Umang/Aarogya Setu app.
Register using your mobile number and enter the OTP.
Add family members and enter their ID details.
Search for nearby vaccination centers using PIN code.
Book an available slot and download the certificate post-vaccination.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: eSanjeevani OPD
shortDescription: National teleconsultation service for seeking medical advice from doctors remotely.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen needing non-emergency medical advice.

fees.details: Consultations are completely free of charge.

stepsToApply: Download the eSanjeevani app or visit the web portal.
Verify your mobile number via OTP to register.
Generate a token and wait for your turn in the digital queue.
Connect with the doctor via video call when called.
Download the digital e-prescription after the consultation.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: CGHS Beneficiary Services
shortDescription: Comprehensive healthcare facilities for central government employees and pensioners.
eligibility: Central government employees, pensioners, and their dependent family members.

fees.details: Monthly contribution deducted from salary/pension based on pay matrix level.

stepsToApply: Fill out the CGHS application form available online or at the department.
Attach required proof of residence, pay slip, and family details.
Submit the verified form to the Additional Director of CGHS.
Collect the plastic CGHS beneficiary cards when printed.

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
title: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
shortDescription: Maternity benefit program providing cash incentives for pregnant women and lactating mothers.
eligibility: Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers for their first living child.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card of Applicant and Husband
stepsToApply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or approved health facility within 150 days of LMP.
Submit Form 1-A with Aadhaar and bank details to receive the first installment.
Submit Form 1-B after one ante-natal check-up (after 6 months of pregnancy) for the second installment.
Submit Form 1-C after childbirth and first cycle of immunization for the final installment.

title: ESIC Health Scheme
shortDescription: Social security and health insurance scheme for Indian workers providing full medical care.
eligibility: Employees in non-seasonal factories/establishments earning up to ₹21,000 per month.

fees.details: 0.75% of wages from employee, 3.25% from employer.

stepsToApply: Employer registers the establishment on the ESIC portal.
Employer registers the eligible employee on the portal.
An insurance number is generated automatically.
Employee provides family details and biometrics at the ESIC branch to get the Pehchan card.
Access medical care at ESIC dispensaries and hospitals using the card.

title: PM Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana
shortDescription: Quality generic medicines at affordable prices through special Kendra outlets.

fees.details: Medicines are priced 50% to 90% lower than branded equivalents.

requiredDocuments: Valid Medical Prescription (for schedule H/X drugs)
stepsToApply: Use the app to locate the nearest Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendra (PMBJK).
Search for the required generic medicines or their branded equivalents.
Visit the Kendra with your prescription.
Purchase the affordable generic medicines directly.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
shortDescription: Safe motherhood intervention promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
eligibility: BPL pregnant women delivering in government health facilities or accredited private institutions.

fees.details: Beneficiaries receive cash assistance; no fees apply.

stepsToApply: Register pregnancy with the local ASHA worker or at the nearest Sub-Centre.
Undergo at least three antenatal check-ups.
Deliver the baby at a government facility or accredited private hospital.
Submit delivery documents and bank details to the medical officer.
Cash assistance is transferred via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Nikshay Poshan Yojana
shortDescription: Direct Benefit Transfer scheme for nutritional support to Tuberculosis (TB) patients.
eligibility: All notified TB patients receiving treatment in public or private sectors.

fees.details: Beneficiaries receive ₹500/month for the duration of treatment.

stepsToApply: Get diagnosed and notified on the Nikshay portal by the treating medical facility.
Submit bank account and Aadhaar details to the healthcare provider.
The health facility updates the details on the Nikshay portal.
Verification is completed by the District TB Officer.
Funds are directly credited to the patient's bank account monthly.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Mission Indradhanush
shortDescription: Routine immunization campaign to cover partially and unvaccinated children and pregnant women.
eligibility: Children up to 2 years of age and pregnant women.

fees.details: All vaccines under the program are free of cost.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar or Parent's ID (optional but recommended)
stepsToApply: Identify the dates for the Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI) drive in your district.
Visit the nearest local Anganwadi Centre, Sub-Centre, or designated vaccination camp.
Present the MCP card to the health worker.
Worker updates the immunization record physically and digitally (U-WIN).

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK)
shortDescription: Early identification and early intervention for children from birth to 18 years for 4Ds (Defects, Diseases, Deficiencies, Development delays).
eligibility: All children from 0 to 18 years enrolled in government/govt-aided schools and Anganwadis.

fees.details: Screening and subsequent tertiary treatments are completely free.

stepsToApply: Attend the health screening conducted by mobile health teams at the Anganwadi or school.
If a condition is detected, receive a referral card from the health team.
Visit the designated District Early Intervention Center (DEIC) or public health facility.
Receive free medical or surgical treatment as required.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA)
shortDescription: Assured, comprehensive, and quality antenatal care to pregnant women on the 9th of every month.
eligibility: Pregnant women in their 2nd and 3rd trimesters.

fees.details: No cost for check-ups, ultrasounds, or medicines on the designated day.

stepsToApply: Visit any government health facility or empanelled private clinic on the 9th of the month.
Undergo diagnostic tests (blood, urine, BP, ultrasound).
Consult with the medical officer/OBGYN specialist.
Receive color-coded stickers on the MCP card based on risk level and free supplements.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN)
shortDescription: Financial assistance to BPL patients suffering from major life-threatening diseases for treatment at super specialty hospitals.
eligibility: BPL citizens needing treatment for specified life-threatening diseases in government super specialty hospitals.

fees.details: Grant-based financial assistance up to ₹15 lakhs.

requiredDocuments: Medical Certificate & Estimate from Government Hospital
stepsToApply: Obtain an estimated cost of treatment from the treating doctor at a government super specialty hospital.
Fill out the RAN application form.
Get the form forwarded and recommended by the Medical Superintendent of the hospital.
Submit the application to the Ministry of Health or the hospital's revolving fund desk.
Upon approval, funds are released directly to the Medical Superintendent of the hospital.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakaram (JSSK)
shortDescription: Entitles all pregnant women and sick infants to completely free delivery and treatment in public health institutions.
eligibility: All pregnant women delivering in public health institutions and sick infants up to 1 year.

fees.details: Free drugs, diagnostics, diet, and transport.

stepsToApply: Register the pregnancy at a public health facility.
Call the toll-free number (102/108) to avail of free transport to the hospital during labor.
Get admitted to the government hospital without paying any admission fees.
Receive free delivery (including C-section), drugs, consumables, and food.
Utilize free drop-back transport to home after discharge.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Tele-MANAS
shortDescription: National Telemental Health Programme offering 24x7 free tele-mental health services.
eligibility: Any individual seeking mental health support or counseling in India.
stepsToApply: Dial the toll-free number 14416 or 1-800-891-4416 from any phone.
Select your preferred language through the IVRS.
Speak to a trained counselor for basic psychological support.
If required, get referred to a clinical psychologist or psychiatrist (Tier 2).
For severe cases, receive guidance for in-person consultation at district centers.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: National Dialysis Programme
shortDescription: Free dialysis services for BPL patients at district hospitals under the PMNDP.
eligibility: All citizens; BPL patients receive services completely free of cost.

fees.details: Free for BPL; heavily subsidized fixed rates for non-BPL patients.

stepsToApply: Get a diagnosis and dialysis prescription from a recognized medical officer/nephrologist.
Visit the Dialysis unit at the nearest District Hospital.
Submit the BPL card and prescription at the registration counter.
Undergo the hemodialysis procedure at the scheduled times.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: eRaktKosh (Blood Bank Management)
shortDescription: Centralized blood bank management system to check real-time availability of blood components.
eligibility: Anyone looking for blood or wishing to donate blood.

fees.details: App usage is free; blood processing fees apply per NACO guidelines.

stepsToApply: Visit the eRaktKosh portal or download the mobile app.
Select 'Check Blood Availability' and enter state, district, and blood group.
View the list of nearby blood banks with real-time stock.
Visit the selected blood bank with the doctor's requisition form.
Complete the cross-matching process and receive the blood.

title: Ayush Sanjivani
shortDescription: Initiative to promote and document the use of traditional Indian medicine for immunity and wellness.
eligibility: All citizens interested in or using AYUSH systems for health.

fees.details: App and guidelines are free to access.

stepsToApply: Download the Ayush Sanjivani app from the app store.
Register using a mobile number and OTP.
Access guidelines and advisories on immunity-boosting measures.
Participate in surveys documenting the usage of AYUSH remedies.
Find nearby AYUSH wellness centers and practitioners.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana (RVY)
shortDescription: Provides physical aids and assisted-living devices for senior citizens suffering from age-related disabilities.
eligibility: Senior citizens (above 60 years) belonging to BPL category or monthly income below ₹15,000.

fees.details: Devices (wheelchairs, hearing aids, spectacles, etc.) provided completely free.

stepsToApply: Wait for the assessment camp organized by ALIMCO or the district administration in your area.
Visit the camp with necessary age, income, and identity proofs.
Undergo a medical assessment by doctors present at the camp.
Get registered for the required assistive device.
Collect the prescribed aid at the subsequent distribution camp.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NHM Free Drugs Service Initiative
shortDescription: Provision of essential medicines free of cost in public health facilities to reduce out-of-pocket expenses.
eligibility: All patients visiting public healthcare facilities (PHCs, CHCs, District Hospitals).

fees.details: Essential medicines listed under the state formulary are provided at zero cost.

requiredDocuments: Prescription from a Government Medical Officer
stepsToApply: Consult a doctor at a government health facility.
Get the required medicines prescribed on the official hospital slip.
Present the prescription at the in-house hospital pharmacy counter.
Collect the prescribed essential medicines free of charge.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NHM Free Diagnostics Service Initiative
shortDescription: Provides free essential diagnostic services like pathology, radiology, and imaging tests in public hospitals.
eligibility: All patients visiting government health facilities.

fees.details: Listed essential lab tests and imaging are free.

requiredDocuments: Diagnostic requisition form signed by a government doctor
stepsToApply: Visit a government hospital and undergo a medical checkup.
Receive a test recommendation from the attending physician.
Take the requisition slip to the hospital's internal laboratory or imaging unit.
Undergo the required test and collect the reports on the specified date.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Ayushman Arogya Mandir (Health & Wellness Centres)
shortDescription: Decentralized primary healthcare centers providing comprehensive healthcare, yoga wellness, and diagnostic services closer to communities.
eligibility: Open to all residents within the community catchment area.

fees.details: Primary consultations, wellness activities, and medicines are completely free.

requiredDocuments: ABHA Card or Aadhaar Card (recommended for history tracking)
stepsToApply: Locate the nearest Ayushman Arogya Mandir in your village or urban block.
Walk in during working hours for a consultation or screening.
Register using your ABHA ID or basic demographic info.
Avail of outpatient care, lifestyle/wellness counseling, or free diagnostics.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Surakshit Matritva Aashwasan (SUMAN)
shortDescription: Assures dignified, respectful, and quality healthcare at zero cost for every woman and newborn visiting public facilities.
eligibility: All pregnant women, mothers up to 6 months post-delivery, and sick newborns.

fees.details: Complete zero-expense guarantee for delivery and complications.

stepsToApply: Visit any SUMAN-notified public health facility during pregnancy.
Register at the antenatal care counter.
Avail guaranteed services including at least 4 ANC checkups, free delivery, and referral transport.
Report any grievance or out-of-pocket demand to the dedicated SUMAN toll-free desk if required.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Financial Support for Rare Diseases
shortDescription: One-time financial assistance for patients suffering from rare diseases for treatment at designated Centers of Excellence.
eligibility: Patients suffering from rare diseases categorized under the national policy, treated at Centers of Excellence.

fees.details: Financial assistance of up to ₹50 lakhs is provided directly to the hospital.

requiredDocuments: Medical Diagnostic Report from a Center of Excellence
Income Certificate (if applicable for priority groups)
stepsToApply: Get evaluated at one of the government-designated Centers of Excellence (CoE) for rare diseases.
The institutional clinical committee confirms the condition and develops a treatment layout.
Apply online through the digital portal setup for rare diseases with the help of the CoE coordinator.
The application is processed by the Ministry, and funds are disbursed directly to the designated CoE.

title: AMRIT Pharmacy Scheme
shortDescription: Outlets providing highly subsidized medicines, implants, and surgical consumables for cancer and cardiovascular diseases.
eligibility: Open to all patients with valid specialist prescriptions.

fees.details: Discounts ranging from 60% to 90% on maximum retail prices.

requiredDocuments: Valid Medical Prescription from a Registered Medical Practitioner
stepsToApply: Locate an AMRIT pharmacy store, typically situated inside major government medical colleges and hospitals.
Present your prescription for specialized cancer, cardiac, or chronic disease drugs.
The pharmacist checks availability and calculates the heavily discounted rate.
Complete the payment and collect the medicines along with a detailed invoice.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: National Organ and Tissue Transplant Organisation
title: NOTTO Organ Donor Registration
shortDescription: National registry for citizens to pledge their organs and tissues for transplantation after death.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 18 years or above (or minors with parental consent).

fees.details: Voluntary pledge registration is completely free.

stepsToApply: Visit the official NOTTO web portal.
Click on the 'Register as Organ/Tissue Donor' tab.
Fill out Form 7 online with your demographic data and organ preferences.
Verify your details using Aadhaar-linked OTP.
Download and print your official National Organ Donor Card.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NACO HIV/AIDS Care & Support
shortDescription: Provides free counseling, testing, and lifelong Anti-Retroviral Therapy (ART) medicines through specialized centers.
eligibility: Any person seeking HIV screening or living with HIV/AIDS in India.

fees.details: Testing, psychological counseling, and ART medicines are provided free of cost.

requiredDocuments: Consent Form (filled at the testing center)
stepsToApply: Visit a nearby Integrated Counseling and Testing Centre (ICTC) located within public hospitals.
Undergo pre-test counseling and give a blood sample.
Collect reports confidentially after post-test counseling.
If diagnosed positive, register at the designated Anti-Retroviral Therapy (ART) center.
Undergo clinical evaluation and receive regular free ART medication supplies.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Rashtriya Kishor Swasthya Karyakram (RKSK)
shortDescription: Holistic healthcare program for adolescents targeting nutrition, mental health, menstrual hygiene, and substance abuse counseling.
eligibility: Adolescents between the ages of 10 and 19 years.

fees.details: All clinics, counseling, and sanitary pad distribution systems are free.

requiredDocuments: School ID card or age certificate (recommended for record registration)
stepsToApply: Visit the closest 'Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic' (AFHC) or Saathiya Centre.
Consult with a peer educator or an assigned institutional counselor.
Participate in weekly iron-folic acid supplementation or wellness tracking screenings.
Collect structural health resources, like sanitary napkins or informational booklets, directly from the kiosk.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: National Viral Hepatitis Control Program (NVHCP)
shortDescription: National service providing free diagnostic evaluation and comprehensive treatment for Hepatitis B and C infections.
eligibility: Any person diagnosed with or suspected of having viral hepatitis infections.

fees.details: Screening tests, viral load assessment, and antiviral medicines are completely free.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card or relevant photo identity check
stepsToApply: Visit a designated NVHCP treatment center situated at public medical colleges or district hospitals.
Undergo rapid diagnostic screening for Hepatitis B & C.
If positive, proceed to confirmatory viral load quantification testing.
Get registered under the NVHCP patient registration register.
Collect your regular monthly dosage of prescribed direct-acting antiviral medications from the treatment desk.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly
title: NPHCE Elderly Care Services
shortDescription: Dedicated healthcare framework offering promotional, preventive, curative, and rehabilitative services custom-tailored for senior citizens.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 60 years and above.

fees.details: Specialized geriatric OPD services and medical aids are provided free.

requiredDocuments: Age proof document (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, or Birth Certificate)
stepsToApply: Visit a district hospital containing a dedicated NPHCE Geriatric Clinic.
Register your details at the senior citizens special counter.
Consult with a geriatric medical specialist.
Access dedicated laboratory investigations, physical rehabilitation, or specialized weekly clinics without general queues.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: National Programme for Control of Blindness
title: NPCBVI Cataract Surgery Services
shortDescription: National program focusing on reducing visual impairments by providing free cataract identification screening and operational surgeries.
eligibility: Individuals suffering from visually blinding cataracts, with priority to marginalized and rural communities.

fees.details: Screening, surgical operation, intraocular lens installation, and spectacles are free.

requiredDocuments: Income status verification (if applying via specific camp systems)
stepsToApply: Attend an official NPCBVI mobile eye screening camp or go directly to the district hospital ophthalmology unit.
Undergo professional visual acuity checks and ophthalmic validation mapping.
If diagnosed with operable cataracts, schedule a surgical date.
Get admitted to the operative block for lens replacement processing.
Collect post-operative tracking medicines and free corrective glasses upon medical release.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
department: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
title: Unique Disability ID (UDID) Health Benefits
shortDescription: A unified nationwide identity card for persons with disabilities to streamline healthcare allocations and medical benefits.
eligibility: Any individual possessing a certified medical disability condition.

fees.details: Card processing and assessment validation carry no cost elements.

requiredDocuments: Disability Certificate issued by a competent Medical Board
stepsToApply: Visit the digital Swavlamban online platform portal.
Click on 'Apply for Disability Certificate & UDID Card'.
Complete the structured application form detailing demographic data and personal medical impairments.
Upload digital copies of required identity proofs and active medical certificates.
Attend the assigned date validation at the local government hospital for medical board review, post which the physical card is dispatched.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: National Tobacco Cessation Quitline
shortDescription: A toll-free telephone-based counseling service to help citizens overcome addiction to smoking and smokeless tobacco products.
eligibility: Any citizen seeking help to quit tobacco usage.
stepsToApply: Dial the national toll-free quitline number 1800-11-2356.
Choose your preferred language for interaction.
Register your details anonymously with the intake counselor.
Receive personalized behavior cessation therapy and planning schedules.
Engage in proactively managed follow-up tracking calls over the next few weeks.

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
title: POSHAN Tracker Subsidized Nutrition
shortDescription: Real-time monitoring system providing nutritional tracking and supplementary food delivery for children and mothers.
eligibility: Children under 6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

fees.details: Take-Home Rations (THR) and Hot Cooked Meals are provided free.

stepsToApply: Visit the local area Anganwadi Centre.
Provide identity details to register the beneficiary on the Poshan Tracker system.
Undergo monthly height and weight checks by the Anganwadi worker.
Collect designated Take-Home Ration packages every month.
Ensure real-time attendance registration for children to get Hot Cooked Meals.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NPCDCS Chronic Disease Screening
shortDescription: National program managing mass screening and primary treatment facilities for cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases.
eligibility: All citizens, with special focus on individuals over 30 years old.

fees.details: Basic blood sugar checks, blood pressure monitoring, and screening tests are free.

requiredDocuments: Basic Demographic Identity Proof (Aadhaar or Voter card)
stepsToApply: Visit the NCD cell or clinic at the nearest primary health center or district hospital.
Request a generic non-communicable disease screening checklist validation.
Undergo physiological evaluations including random blood sugar tracking and blood pressure measurement.
Participate in physical cancer screening examinations if recommended by the officer.
Receive basic counseling and long-term diagnostic tracking books if risk factors emerge.

ministry: Ministry of Consumers Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
department: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India
title: FSSAI Food Safety Training (FoSTaC)
shortDescription: Mandatory and voluntary health training certification courses covering clean food handling for food businesses.
eligibility: Food business operators, catering managers, students, and home bakers.

fees.details: Varies by training partner, typically ranges between ₹500 and ₹2000.

requiredDocuments: Active email ID and digital passport photo
stepsToApply: Open the web portal interface for FoSTaC.
Click on 'Register as Trainee' and build your user account profiles.
Select an active course category matching your needs (Basic, Advanced, or Special).
Choose a convenient slot provided by verified training partners and make the payment.
Attend the classroom sessions, pass the baseline exam, and download your Food Safety Supervisor Certificate.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: LaQshya Labour Room Certification
shortDescription: Quality improvement program optimizing public hospital labor rooms to ensure safe and complication-free childbirth environments.
eligibility: All pregnant women delivering inside public health structures.

fees.details: Integrated structural services under the quality standard umbrella cost nothing.

stepsToApply: Identify nearby government medical colleges or district hospitals carrying a 'LaQshya' quality badge.
Get admitted to the high-dependency delivery unit during early labor stages.
Avail standard protocol-driven intrapartum monitoring checks overseen by trained labor nurses.
Benefit from private labor pods, professional triage processing, and zero illegal out-of-pocket charges.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NLEP Leprosy Medical & Welfare Benefits
shortDescription: Provides early diagnosis screening, free Multi-Drug Therapy (MDT) medication, and reconstructive surgery allowances.
eligibility: Any individual diagnosed with leprosy skin patch variations or physical nerve thickening.

fees.details: MDT drug course is free; patients receive a cash allowance for reconstructive procedures.

requiredDocuments: Medical Diagnostic Report from a Leprosy Officer
Bank Account passbook details for structural allowance transfer
stepsToApply: Report visible anesthetic skin lesions to the medical officer at any public healthcare clinic.
Undergo skin smear laboratory diagnostics to map structural bacterial presence.
Receive initial categorization and log into the NLEP patient master tracking books.
Collect free monthly MDT blister packs regularly.
Apply for structural disability deformity surgeries if needed to secure financial welfare support.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: National Ambulance Service (102 / 108 Helpline)
shortDescription: Emergency healthcare transportation network providing free transit to critical patients and mothers.
eligibility: Any citizen facing acute trauma emergencies, labor onset, or childhood illnesses.

fees.details: Completely free transit provisioning when using centralized helpline links.

requiredDocuments: None required during emergency transport processing.
stepsToApply: Dial the emergency phone digits 108 (for medical/accident emergencies) or 102 (for maternity cases).
Relay precise geographic tracking details and user condition metrics to the helpline agent.
Keep lines clear while the nearest GPS-mapped emergency vehicle shifts towards you.
Board the vehicle to receive basic pre-hospital stabilizer treatments during transit to the nearest hospital.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NMHP Community Mental Health Services
shortDescription: District-level mental health services offering institutional psychiatric counseling and free psychiatric drugs.
eligibility: Any citizen experiencing psychological stress or mental illness conditions.

fees.details: Outpatient consultation and baseline psychotropic medication courses carry zero fee markers.

requiredDocuments: Identity Document (Optional for basic counseling sessions)
stepsToApply: Visit the dedicated District Mental Health Programme (DMHP) clinic block inside your district hospital.
Complete an initial psychological profiling questionnaire via the clinic desk.
Consult with an active clinical psychologist or consulting psychiatrist.
Attend follow-up therapeutic sessions and collect tracking medication slips for collection at the internal pharmacy kiosk.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme
title: NVBDCP Vector Borne Disease Treatment
shortDescription: Integrated platform providing diagnostic tools and medicines for malaria, dengue, kala-azar, and filariasis.
eligibility: Any person showing typical symptoms like periodic fever, body aches, or chills.

fees.details: Rapid diagnostic test kits and complete drug regimens are completely free.

requiredDocuments: None (Symptomatic assessment gets rapid treatment validation)
stepsToApply: Approach a local ASHA worker or public health post upon experiencing unexplained high fever conditions.
Undergo a finger-prick blood test using a Rapid Diagnostic Kit (RDK) or prepare a blood slide.
Receive instantaneous diagnostic confirmation within 15 minutes.
Collect immediate age-appropriate blister pack courses of Artesunate or Chloroquine based on guidelines.
Coordinate vector management checking if localized cluster outbreaks occur.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: IHIP Infectious Disease Reporting
shortDescription: Digital interface allowing community members and health units to instantly report localized infectious disease outbreaks.
eligibility: Healthcare workers, institutional private practitioners, and community members tracking outbreak signatures.

fees.details: Reporting platform operations carry zero charges.

requiredDocuments: Lab confirmation report detailing the pathogen signature (if available)
stepsToApply: Open the Integrated Health Information Platform portal interface.
Navigate to the community outbreak report window.
Input localized details regarding specific symptom clusters observed (e.g., sudden waterborne illness, fever spike).
Submit the digital form to prompt automated tracking responses from the district epidemiological investigation team.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: Sanjeevani Teleconsultation for Pensioners
shortDescription: Tailored online medical consultation modules linked to CGHS panels customized to provide swift digital care for retired personnel.
eligibility: Retired government employees carrying active pension card tracking.

fees.details: Specialized virtual queues are free to use.

stepsToApply: Log into the digital eSanjeevani online hub using your primary mobile numbers.
Select the dedicated 'Pensioner Care Consultation Room' node option.
Provide validation details linked to your PPO register indices.
Connect with a specialty physician via high-definition video call systems.
Download certified system e-prescriptions valid across global retail drug outlets.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: National Iron Plus Initiative (NIPI)
shortDescription: Comprehensive strategy providing Iron and Folic Acid (IFA) supplementation to completely prevent and manage anemia across age groups.
eligibility: Infants, adolescents, women of reproductive age, and pregnant/lactating individuals.

fees.details: All preventative syrup distributions and therapeutic tablets are free.

requiredDocuments: MCP Card (for pregnant women) or School Enrollment Ledger profile
stepsToApply: Visit your neighborhood Anganwadi center or public primary school layout.
Participate in designated weekly or bi-weekly health checkups.
Collect age-appropriate Iron-Folic Acid packages (pink, blue, or red tablets depending on target group criteria).
Undergo hemoglobin screening if continuous physical fatigue signs persist.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India
title: FSSAI Food Product Licensing (FOSCOS)
shortDescription: Cloud-based framework for food business operators to acquire mandatory food safety operational licenses and hygiene registration cards.
eligibility: All individuals or corporate groups producing, selling, storing, or transferring food products.

fees.details: Standard registration starts at ₹100/year; State/Central licenses vary from ₹2000 to ₹7500/year.

requiredDocuments: Identity Proof and Address Verification documents
Layout blueprint of manufacturing plant blueprints
Water analysis safety validation report certificates
stepsToApply: Access the digital FOSCOS online framework application hub.
Enter your setup operational parameters to check baseline license tier eligibility rules.
Fill out the comprehensive application profile details (Form A or Form B).
Upload verified document scans alongside factory setup proof matrices.
Submit the state processing fees online and await structural verification checks by the local Food Safety Officer.

title: e-Charak (Medicinal Plants Portal)
shortDescription: Digital marketplace showcase helping farmers and herbal experts exchange authentic medicinal herbs and wellness items safely.
eligibility: Farmers growing medicinal plants, traditional wellness practitioners, and alternative pharmaceutical buyers.

fees.details: Registration and market showcase listings are free.

requiredDocuments: Land Ownership papers or Trade License registers
stepsToApply: Open the online e-Charak web portal space.
Create a user account profile categorizing yourself as a 'Seller' or 'Buyer'.
Log detailed item listings including verified images and botanical names.
Connect securely with potential buyers or contract farming units.
Execute safe commercial exchanges without deep middleman market margins.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: Voluntary Blood Donation Voluntary Portal
shortDescription: Centralized interface linking willing blood donors with certified public blood banks during emergencies.
eligibility: Healthy individuals aged 18 to 65 years weighing at least 45 kg.

fees.details: Voluntary donation platforms carry zero fees; donors receive health validation certificates.

requiredDocuments: Valid Government Photo ID Card (Aadhaar, Driving License, etc.)
stepsToApply: Access the eRaktKosh online platform interface.
Click on the 'Register as a Voluntary Donor' tab link.
Fill out your health profiling metrics alongside blood type markers.
Select a convenient neighborhood donation camp or specific government blood storage branch.
Complete basic hemoglobin evaluations at the center prior to donating blood securely.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: NCDC Diagnostic Lab Services
shortDescription: Specialized laboratory diagnostics for complex zoonotic, bacterial, and viral pathogens under national surveillance protocols.
eligibility: Patients carrying specific clinical reference notes from institutional medical boards or state departments.

fees.details: Highly subsidized baseline pricing or free if handled under national epidemiological tracking drives.

requiredDocuments: Official reference sheet stamped by a government medical college hospital board
Patient dynamic case clinical files summary history documentation
stepsToApply: Secure a specialized diagnostic screening referral note from a tertiary healthcare center doctor.
Visit the designated sample handling reception point at an NCDC regional reference lab branch.
Submit sample extractions under exact cold-chain tracking frameworks.
Monitor status check codes via the digital notification trackers provided.
Collect final molecular sequencing results or specialized culture testing matrices.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
title: CDSCO Sugam Drug Portal Registration
shortDescription: Official portal managing authorization reviews, clinical study updates, and manufacturing clearances for critical healthcare products.
eligibility: Pharmaceutical researchers, corporate developers, clinical research organizations, and import firms.

fees.details: Pricing matches specific statutory clearance schedules defined by the drug rules.

requiredDocuments: Corporate registration profiles or Manufacturing Site operational permits
Chemical dossier validation files and analytical stability test data logs
Detailed clinical trail monitoring reports summary parameters
stepsToApply: Open the primary CDSCO Sugam online operational application portal.
Create a secure enterprise profile using verified organization certificates.
Select the relevant application node (e.g., import permit, clinical trial submission).
Fill out technical specification data cards and link digital testing data folders.
Complete statutory system payments online to initiate review panels by central drug inspectors.
